document_title: Diploma in Teacher Education - Child Development and Psychology Curriculum

modules.strand: 1.0 Introduction to Child Growth and Development

sub_strands.name: 1.1 Concept of Child Growth and Development
specific_learning_outcomes: Examine terminologies related to child growth and development
Explain the concept of child growth and development
Analyse the principles of child growth and development
suggested_learning_experiences: Teacher trainees define key terms such as growth, development, maturation, and learning.
Trainees discuss the differences and interrelationships between growth and development.
Case studies on developmental milestones are analysed.

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to examine terminologies related to child growth and development

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Clearly examines terminologies related to child growth and development
levels.Meets Expectation: Examines terminologies related to child growth and development
levels.Approaches Expectation: Partially examines terminologies related to child growth and development
levels.Below Expectation: Requires assistance to examine terminologies related to child growth and development

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to explain the concept of child growth and development

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Comprehensively explains the concept of child growth and development
levels.Meets Expectation: Explains the concept of child growth and development
levels.Approaches Expectation: Partially explains the concept of child growth and development
levels.Below Expectation: Only with support, explains the concept of child growth and development

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to analyse the principles of child growth and development

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Accurately analyses all principles of child growth and development
levels.Meets Expectation: Analyses the principles of child growth and development
levels.Approaches Expectation: Partially analyses the principles of child growth and development
levels.Below Expectation: Only with guidance, analyses the principles of child growth and development

sub_strands.name: 1.2 Stages of Child Development
specific_learning_outcomes: Examine changes in children from infancy to adolescence
Illustrate the characteristics of children from infancy to adolescence
suggested_learning_experiences: Trainees map out physical, cognitive, social, and emotional changes across developmental stages.
Role-play scenarios depicting typical behaviours at different ages.
Observe children in real or video settings and document their developmental traits.
core_competencies: Learning to learn and reflective practice

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to examine changes in children from infancy to adolescence

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Comprehensively examine changes in children from infancy to adolescence
levels.Meets Expectation: Analyses changes in children from infancy to adolescence
levels.Approaches Expectation: Partially analyses changes in children from infancy to adolescence
levels.Below Expectation: Leaves out many details in analyses of changes in children from infancy to adolescence

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to illustrate the characteristics of children from infancy to adolescence

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Distinctively illustrates the characteristics of children from infancy to adolescence
levels.Meets Expectation: Illustrates the characteristics of children from infancy to adolescence
levels.Approaches Expectation: Illustrates some characteristics of children from infancy to adolescence
levels.Below Expectation: Illustrates unclear characteristics of children from infancy to adolescence

sub_strands.name: 1.3 Factors Influencing Child Development
specific_learning_outcomes: Identify factors that influence child growth and development
Assess the impact of heredity and environment on child development
suggested_learning_experiences: Debate nature vs. nurture influences on development.
Research and present on how malnutrition, disease, parenting styles, and socio-economic status affect development.
Analyse case studies where environmental deprivation has impacted development.

sub_strands.name: 1.4 Individual Differences in Children
specific_learning_outcomes: Examine individual differences among children
Appreciate the importance of recognizing individual differences in a classroom setting
suggested_learning_experiences: Trainees complete personality or learning style inventories.
Discuss strategies for accommodating diverse learners in the classroom.
Create profiles of hypothetical learners with varying abilities and interests.

sub_strands.name: 1.5 Personality Development
specific_learning_outcomes: Examine personality development in relation to child growth and development
Propose personality development challenges experienced by learners
suggested_learning_experiences: Study major theories of personality (e.g., Freud, Erikson).
Discuss common personality issues in school-aged children (e.g., shyness, aggression, low self-esteem).
Develop intervention plans for addressing personality-related challenges.

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to examine personality development in relation to child growth and development

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Clearly examines personality development in relation to child growth and development
levels.Meets Expectation: Examines personality development in relation to child growth and development
levels.Approaches Expectation: Partially examines personality development in relation to child growth and development
levels.Below Expectation: Examines personality development in relation to child growth and development with details that require clarity

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to propose the personality development challenges experienced by learners

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Accurately proposes the personality development challenges experienced by learners
levels.Meets Expectation: Proposes the personality development challenges experienced by learners
levels.Approaches Expectation: Proposes few personality development challenges experienced by learners that are inaccurate
levels.Below Expectation: Many proposed personality development challenges experienced by learners require clarity

sub_strands.name: 2.1 Psychoanalytic Theories
specific_learning_outcomes: Examine psychoanalytic theories of growth and development
Analyse Sigmund Freud’s theory of psychosexual development
Analyse Erik Erikson’s theory of psychosocial development
suggested_learning_experiences: Compare and contrast Freud’s and Erikson’s stages of development.
Apply theoretical concepts to real-life examples of children’s behaviour.
Critique the relevance of psychoanalytic theories in modern educational contexts.

sub_strands.name: 2.2 Cognitive Development Theories
specific_learning_outcomes: Examine cognitive development theories
Analyse Jean Piaget’s theory of cognitive development
Analyse Lev Vygotsky’s theory of sociocultural development
suggested_learning_experiences: Conduct experiments or demonstrations illustrating Piagetian concepts (e.g., conservation tasks).
Design classroom activities based on Vygotsky’s Zone of Proximal Development (ZPD) and scaffolding.
Compare Piaget’s and Vygotsky’s views on language and thought.

sub_strands.name: 2.3 Behavioural Theories
specific_learning_outcomes: Examine behavioural theories of growth and development
Analyse Ivan Pavlov’s classical conditioning theory
Analyse B.F. Skinner’s operant conditioning theory
suggested_learning_experiences: Demonstrate classical conditioning using simple stimuli (e.g., bell and light).
Design a token economy system for classroom management based on operant conditioning.
Discuss ethical considerations in applying behavioural modification techniques.

sub_strands.name: 2.4 Humanistic Theories
specific_learning_outcomes: Examine humanistic theories of growth and development
Analyse Abraham Maslow’s hierarchy of needs
Analyse Carl Rogers’ person-centred theory
suggested_learning_experiences: Map Maslow’s hierarchy onto students' needs in school environments.
Role-play counselling sessions using Rogerian principles (empathy, unconditional positive regard).
Discuss how schools can foster self-actualization in learners.

sub_strands.name: 3.1 Classical Conditioning
specific_learning_outcomes: Describe Ivan Pavlov’s experiment on classical conditioning
Demonstrate classical conditioning in a learning environment
suggested_learning_experiences: Recreate simplified versions of Pavlov’s dog experiment using sound and visual cues.
Identify examples of classical conditioning in everyday school life (e.g., anxiety associated with tests).

sub_strands.name: 3.2 Operant Conditioning
specific_learning_outcomes: Describe B.F. Skinner’s concept of operant conditioning
Demonstrate operant conditioning theory in a learning environment
suggested_learning_experiences: Design reinforcement schedules (fixed ratio, variable interval) for classroom use.
Implement positive/negative reinforcement and punishment in simulated teaching scenarios.

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to demonstrate operant conditioning theory in a learning environment

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Comprehensively demonstrates operant conditioning theory in a learning environment
levels.Meets Expectation: Demonstrates operant conditioning theory in a learning environment
levels.Approaches Expectation: Leaves out some details when applying operant conditioning theory
levels.Below Expectation: Leaves out many details when applying operant conditioning theory

sub_strands.name: 3.3 Social Learning Theory
specific_learning_outcomes: Explain Albert Bandura’s social learning theory
Demonstrate observational learning in a classroom setting
suggested_learning_experiences: Show videos of Bandura’s Bobo doll experiment.
Conduct role modelling exercises where trainees model desired behaviours for peers.
Discuss the role of media and peer influence on children’s learning.

sub_strands.name: 3.4 Cognitive Learning Theories
specific_learning_outcomes: Examine cognitive learning theories
Analyse information processing theory
Apply cognitive strategies to enhance learning
suggested_learning_experiences: Model the human memory system (sensory, short-term, long-term).
Teach mnemonic devices and other memory aids to peers.
Design lessons incorporating chunking, rehearsal, and elaboration techniques.
core_competencies: Learning to learn and reflective practice

sub_strands.name: 3.5 Constructivist Theories
specific_learning_outcomes: Examine constructivist theories of learning
Analyse Jean Piaget’s and Lev Vygotsky’s contributions to constructivism
Apply constructivist approaches in lesson delivery
suggested_learning_experiences: Design inquiry-based or project-based learning activities.
Facilitate peer teaching sessions using discovery learning methods.
Reflect on personal experiences of constructing knowledge.

sub_strands.name: 4.1 Concept of Guidance and Counselling
specific_learning_outcomes: Describe the concept of guidance and counselling
Differentiate between guidance and counselling
Explain the importance of guidance and counselling for the learner
suggested_learning_experiences: Define key terms and compare definitions across sources.
Invite a professional counsellor to give a guest talk.
Discuss scenarios requiring guidance versus formal counselling.

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to describe the importance of guidance and counselling for the learner

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Clearly describes the importance of guidance and counselling for the learner
levels.Meets Expectation: Describes the importance of guidance and counselling for the learner
levels.Approaches Expectation: Partially describes the importance of guidance and counselling for the learner
levels.Below Expectation: Vaguely describes the importance of guidance and counselling for the learner

sub_strands.name: 4.2 Types of Guidance and Counselling Services
specific_learning_outcomes: Identify types of guidance and counselling services in schools
Explain academic, career, personal-social, and moral counselling
suggested_learning_experiences: Map out the structure of a school counselling program.
Role-play different types of counselling sessions (academic advising, conflict resolution).
Research national policies on school-based counselling services.

sub_strands.name: 4.3 Counselling Skills and Techniques
specific_learning_outcomes: Demonstrate basic counselling skills: active listening, empathy, rapport building
Apply appropriate counselling techniques in simulated situations
suggested_learning_experiences: Practice active listening through paired exercises.
Use reflection, paraphrasing, and summarizing in mock counselling sessions.
Receive feedback on non-verbal communication (eye contact, posture).

sub_strands.name: 4.4 Referral Systems
specific_learning_outcomes: Explain the need for referral systems in guidance and counselling
Identify agencies and professionals to whom learners may be referred
suggested_learning_experiences: Create a directory of local mental health and social service providers.
Discuss ethical boundaries and when to refer a student to external professionals.
Analyse case studies requiring multi-agency collaboration.

sub_strands.name: 4.5 Record Keeping in Guidance and Counselling
specific_learning_outcomes: Explain the importance of record keeping in guidance and counselling
Maintain confidential records of counselling sessions
suggested_learning_experiences: Review sample anonymized counselling logs.
Draft a confidentiality policy for a school counselling office.
Discuss legal and ethical implications of record keeping.

sub_strands.name: 5.1 Concept of Motivation
specific_learning_outcomes: Define motivation
Explain intrinsic and extrinsic motivation
Analyse factors that influence learner motivation
suggested_learning_experiences: Trainees reflect on what motivates them personally as learners.
Classify classroom rewards as intrinsic or extrinsic.
Discuss how autonomy, competence, and relatedness (Self-Determination Theory) drive motivation.

sub_strands.name: 5.2 Theories of Motivation
specific_learning_outcomes: Examine various theories of motivation
Analyse Maslow’s hierarchy of needs in relation to learner motivation
Apply expectancy-value theory and goal-setting theory in educational contexts
suggested_learning_experiences: Map students’ unmet needs to potential motivational barriers.
Set SMART goals for academic achievement and track progress.
Design classroom environments that satisfy basic psychological needs.

sub_strands.name: 5.3 Strategies for Enhancing Motivation
specific_learning_outcomes: Suggest strategies for enhancing learner motivation
Apply motivational strategies in lesson delivery
suggested_learning_experiences: Brainstorm ways to make lessons more engaging and relevant.
Incorporate choice, novelty, and real-world connections into lesson plans.
Use praise, feedback, and recognition effectively during microteaching.

sub_strands.name: 6.1 Concept of Inclusive Education
specific_learning_outcomes: Define inclusive education
Explain the philosophy and rationale for inclusive education
Appreciate the rights of learners with special educational needs
suggested_learning_experiences: Watch documentaries on inclusive classrooms.
Discuss the UN Convention on the Rights of Persons with Disabilities.
Visit inclusive schools or speak with teachers working in inclusive settings.

sub_strands.name: 6.2 Categories of Learners with Special Educational Needs
specific_learning_outcomes: Identify categories of learners with special educational needs: sensory impairments, physical disabilities, learning disabilities, giftedness, emotional and behavioural disorders
Describe characteristics of each category
suggested_learning_experiences: Research and present on specific conditions (e.g., dyslexia, autism, ADHD, visual impairment).
Simulate learning challenges (e.g., wearing earplugs to experience hearing loss).
Interview individuals living with disabilities.

sub_strands.name: 6.3 Identification and Assessment of Learners with Special Needs
specific_learning_outcomes: Explain procedures for identifying and assessing learners with special educational needs
Use screening tools and observation checklists
suggested_learning_experiences: Practice using developmental screening tools.
Observe children and note signs of possible learning difficulties.
Learn about multidisciplinary assessment teams and processes.

sub_strands.name: 6.4 Individualized Education Programme (IEP)
specific_learning_outcomes: Explain the purpose of an Individualized Education Programme (IEP)
Develop an IEP for a learner with special needs
Monitor and evaluate the implementation of an IEP
suggested_learning_experiences: Review sample IEPs.
Draft an IEP for a hypothetical student based on assessment data.
Role-play IEP meetings with parents and specialists.

sub_strands.name: 6.5 Differentiated Instruction
specific_learning_outcomes: Explain the concept of differentiated instruction
Apply differentiated instruction strategies in lesson planning and delivery
suggested_learning_experiences: Modify lesson plans to accommodate different learning styles and readiness levels.
Use tiered assignments, flexible grouping, and learning stations.
Deliver a micro lesson demonstrating differentiation in content, process, or product.

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to apply differentiated method of lesson delivery in a class

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Comprehensively applies differentiated method of lesson delivery in a class
levels.Meets Expectation: Applies differentiated method of lesson delivery in a class
levels.Approaches Expectation: To a certain extent, applies differentiated method of lesson delivery in a class
levels.Below Expectation: Only with guidance, applies differentiated method of lesson delivery in a class

sub_strands.name: 7.1 Physical and Cognitive Changes in Adolescence
specific_learning_outcomes: Describe physical changes during adolescence
Explain cognitive development in adolescence according to Piaget and Vygotsky
suggested_learning_experiences: Chart puberty-related changes in boys and girls.
Discuss abstract thinking, metacognition, and decision-making in teens.
Analyse risk-taking behaviours from a neurological perspective.

sub_strands.name: 7.2 Social and Emotional Development
specific_learning_outcomes: Analyse social and emotional development during adolescence
Discuss identity formation, peer relationships, and emotional regulation
suggested_learning_experiences: Explore Erikson’s stage of Identity vs. Role Confusion.
Discuss the impact of social media on teen self-esteem.
Role-play conflict resolution between friends or family members.

sub_strands.name: 7.3 Challenges Facing Adolescents
specific_learning_outcomes: Identify common challenges facing adolescents: substance abuse, teenage pregnancy, bullying, depression
Suggest intervention strategies for supporting adolescents
suggested_learning_experiences: Conduct surveys or focus groups on adolescent issues.
Develop anti-bullying campaigns or peer mentoring programs.
Invite health educators to discuss sexual and mental health.

sub_strands.name: 8.1 Importance of Play
specific_learning_outcomes: Explain the importance of play in child development
Analyse how play contributes to cognitive, social, emotional, and physical development
suggested_learning_experiences: Observe children at play and document developmental benefits.
Compare structured vs. unstructured play.
Design play-based learning activities for early years.

sub_strands.name: 8.2 Types of Play
specific_learning_outcomes: Identify types of play: solitary, parallel, associative, cooperative, pretend/dramatic play
Demonstrate facilitation of different types of play in early childhood settings
suggested_learning_experiences: Classify observed play behaviours into categories.
Set up play corners (e.g., home corner, block area) and facilitate play.
Discuss teacher roles in supporting but not dominating play.

sub_strands.name: 8.3 Play Materials and Environment
specific_learning_outcomes: Select appropriate play materials and equipment
Create a safe and stimulating play environment
suggested_learning_experiences: Audit a classroom or playground for safety and developmental appropriateness.
Design a play space layout using recycled or low-cost materials.
Evaluate commercial toys for educational value.

subjects.name: Sociological and Philosophical Foundations of Education
Historical and Comparative Foundations of Education

national_goals_of_education: Foster national unity, patriotism, and promote national cohesion.
Increase the level of knowledge of all people to enhance national capacity and strengthen the economy.
Affirm and promote the culture of all Kenyan people.
Provide education and training that enables learners to become self-reliant and contribute to national development.
Promote holistic well-being—physical, emotional, spiritual—for self-reliance.
Enable learners to recognize values, ethics, and morals from religious and cultural perspectives and integrate them with their environment.
Enable learners to use language efficiently as a communication, thinking, and learning tool.
Develop learners’ ability to seek, use, and disseminate information using communication technology.
Develop learners’ ability to solve problems using knowledge, skills, and attitudes.
Enable learners to recognize their potential and harness it for the benefit of family and society.
essence_statement: Education is a critical instrument for economic growth, national unity, and cultural affirmation. It aims to produce citizens capable of contributing to national development through knowledge, skills, and attitudes, while instilling values, ethics, and morals derived from religious and cultural heritage.
